_meta.scenario: North Port FL — marble kitchen countertop restoration

lead.name: Karen M.
lead.surface: Kitchen countertop — L-shaped run with island return
lead.primaryIssue: Etching, dullness, and ring marks from acidic cleaners; minor staining near cooktop
lead.timeline: Within 3–4 weeks — hosting family in July
lead.goal: Restore depth and polish without replacing the slab
lead.budgetNote: Mid-range; compared one contractor quote already
lead.accessNotes: Single-story home, gated community — call at gate

qualification.routing: Site assessment → Architect agent after written scope
qualification.reason: Clear stone type, defined damage, FL service area, realistic timeline, ready to schedule

conversation.text: Hi — I need my marble kitchen counters restored. We're in North Port. Lots of etch marks and they look dull. Can someone come out?

Hi Karen — thanks for reaching out to Master Sanctum Restoration. Marble kitchen counters in North Port are a strong fit for our conservation protocol.

To route this correctly before we schedule a visit, can you confirm:
1. Carrara or another marble? (vein color helps)
2. Approximate counter length / island size?
3. Worst areas — cooktop, sink, or bar seating?
4. Any cracks or chips, or mainly etching/dullness?
5. Your target timeline?

If you have photos (wide shot + close-up of worst etching), that speeds things up. No obligation — I want to make sure assessment is the right next step.

It's Carrara, L-shape with a small island. About 42 sq ft total I think. Main issues are etch rings and dull spots by the cooktop and where we cut lemons. No cracks. We'd like it done before July — family visiting. I can send photos tomorrow.

Perfect — that's exactly the detail we need. Etch and dullness on Carrara is often reversible with proper honing and sealing; cooktop and citrus exposure are common failure points in Florida humidity.

**Recommended next step:** 30–45 minute on-site assessment in North Port. We document finish level, prior products used, and substrate before quoting.

**Please send when ready:**
• 2–3 photos (wide + worst etch areas)
• Property address and gate/access notes
• 2–3 date/time windows that work for you

I'll attach our intake questionnaire at confirmation — you can also download it at sanctum-shortener.is-a.dev/intake

Would Thursday 10:00 AM or Friday 2:00 PM work for a first visit?
Friday at 2 works. Address is [redacted — North Port]. Gate code at arrival. I'll email photos tonight.

Confirmed — I'll send a calendar invite and intake questionnaire shortly. After the visit you'll receive a written scope and estimate under our hand-finish protocol. Looking forward to assessing what the stone remembers.

outputs.emailSubject: Your marble restoration — next step: site assessment in North Port
outputs.emailBody:
Hi Karen,

Thank you for reaching out to Master Sanctum Restoration. From what you've described — Carrara marble, etching and dullness near the cooktop, and your July timeline — this is exactly the kind of commission we assess and restore under conservation protocol.

What I understood:
• Surface: Kitchen countertop — L-shaped run with island (~42 sq ft)
• Location: North Port, FL (gated community — call at gate)
• Main concern: Etch rings, dullness, minor cooktop staining
• Your goal: Restore depth and polish without slab replacement

Recommended next step: a brief on-site assessment (typically 30–45 minutes). We document substrate, veining, prior repairs, and exposure before any tool touches stone.

To confirm your visit, I need:
1. Best date/time windows (2–3 options)
2. Property address and access notes
3. Photos of the surface if you have them (wide + close-up of worst areas)

Once confirmed, you'll receive a calendar invite and our intake questionnaire. After the visit, you'll get a written scope and estimate — no surprise shortcuts, no haste.

Would Friday, June 27 at 2:00 PM or Thursday, June 26 at 10:00 AM work for you?

Protecting What Endures,
Master-Sanctum-Gatekeeper
Master Sanctum Restoration

siteVisitConfirmation.subject: Confirmed — Master Sanctum site assessment · Friday, June 27 at 2:00 PM
siteVisitConfirmation.body:
Hi Karen,

Your on-site stone assessment is confirmed.

Date: Friday, June 27, 2026
Time: 2:00 PM ET
Duration: ~30–45 minutes
Address: North Port, FL (gate — call on arrival)
Assessor: Master Sanctum conservation team

What we'll do on site:
• Document Carrara finish, etching, and cooktop-adjacent staining
• Note prior cleaners/sealers and realistic restoration outcomes
• Discuss timeline before your July family visit
• Outline written scope and estimate next steps

Please before we arrive:
• Clear cooktop and counter work zone as practical
• Have tonight's photos handy on your phone
• Note any HOA or pet access requirements

Attached: Client Intake Questionnaire (if not yet returned)

We look forward to meeting you and assessing what the stone remembers.

Protecting What Endures,
Master-Sanctum-Gatekeeper
Master Sanctum Restoration
Guarded by Marmorax
